interpretation: Short-term allocation issue, delivery delay, or limited capacity constraint.
benchmark_rationale: Conservative stress case. Broadly consistent with documented 2021 semiconductor shortage impacts, including Ford CFO guidance that chip-supplier estimates could imply losing 10–20% of planned Q1 production and IHS Markit estimating nearly 700,000 fewer global vehicles in the quarter.

interpretation: Partial disruption to Taiwan-related semiconductor inputs affecting a meaningful share of semiconductor-linked exposure.
benchmark_rationale: Elevated stress case set near the upper end of Ford’s reported 10–20% planned-production impact range during the 2021 chip shortage. This is an analyst-defined scenario, not a direct measurement of Taiwan supply loss.

interpretation: Major disruption short of full Taiwanese foundry shutdown.
benchmark_rationale: High-impact stress case below a total shutdown. Informed by severe firm-level shortage episodes such as Toyota’s reported 40% September 2021 global production cut and by SIA/BCG warnings that a complete disruption of Taiwanese foundries would be an extreme global event.
